lsp/redis.index_log: bear-make compile_commands.json(10755L)+clangd .cache(9.5M); verified definition(dictAdd)->src/dict.c:493 line-exact via mcp bridge+seed server.c; setup_s=start->index-complete wall est

baseline.verdict: Full. All 6 spine elements + iskvobj/metabits/kvobj-layout/LFU detail/16-byte packing. Cites resolve (object.h:100-112, 96-98, 188-190; server.h:126 for OBJ_SHARED_INTEGERS). Slightly loose: gave refcount fns as object.h:128-130 (actual 128-129) and listed type constants without pinning server.h:856. Reached this via grep+one Read — cheapest (126k ctx) and fastest (61s).

grove.verdict: Full. All 6 spine + extras. Best-grounded: line-exact and correct everywhere — struct object.h:100, robj typedef 115, encoding constants 75, refcount sentinels 96, and uniquely pinned the type constants at server.h:856 (which the reference key omitted). 8 grove tool calls; highest context (300k) and slowest (108s) of the three.

lsp.verdict: Full. All 6 spine + extras, strongest prose on the type/encoding contract and kvobj layout. Cites resolve; incrRefCount/decrRefCount given as object.h:128-129 (exact — more precise than the key's 128-130). Did not pin server.h:856 for type constants. 6 mcp__lsp__definition calls; mid context (213k), 76s.

key_revisions.reason: Spine element 2 (type) located the struct field (object.h:101) but never pinned where the OBJ_STRING/LIST/... type constants are defined. Verified in pinned source: they live in src/server.h:856. grove cited this exactly. Add server.h:856 as the canonical cite for the type-constant values.

L1-redis.verdict: L1-redis: all three arms produce Full answers — every one nails the central type-vs-encoding distinction, refcount sentinels, and LRU/LFU split, with resolvable object.h cites. At this retrieval-difficulty rung answer QUALITY does not separate the tools; the separation is in PROCESS metrics: baseline cheapest+fastest (126k/61s) via grep+read, grove most thorough+best-grounded but priciest (300k/108s, 8 grove calls), lsp in between (213k/76s, 6 semantic definition calls). Note: lsp arm required steering parallel to grove's to engage its tools (see methodology note); without it the agent defaulted to grep.
